beats.activity: Wakes up, showers, throws on clothes and scrolls phone while eating cereal.
beats.reasoning: Jesse sleeps in late on Sundays — 9:15am is typical for him. Folds morning routine into one beat to avoid back-to-back home beats. Full energy to start the day.

beats.activity: Skateboards to Victrola Coffee Roasters for his Sunday shift.
beats.reasoning: Jesse skateboards to work — short neighborhood commute, ~20 min to account for his typical route through Capitol Hill.

beats.activity: Works the morning coffee shift, making espresso drinks and chatting with regulars.
beats.reasoning: Sunday barista shift. Jesse works until 11am, when the FIFA match airs — he's aware of it but not a huge soccer fan, so he doesn't rearrange his schedule.

beats.activity: Steps outside for a quick break, grabs a vape and checks social media.
beats.reasoning: Jesse takes a short break during his shift. The FIFA match is airing but he's working, so he's not watching — not relevant to his Sunday routine.

beats.activity: Finishes his shift, stays late to help restock and chat with coworkers.
beats.reasoning: Jesse's shift extends into early afternoon. He's loyal to his coworkers and doesn't mind staying a bit longer on a sunny day. Light Rail closure doesn't affect him — he skateboards.

beats.activity: Grabs fast food lunch — a burger and fries from a nearby joint.
beats.reasoning: Jesse loves fast food. Lunch break after his shift; 20 min to grab and eat a quick meal in the neighborhood.

beats.activity: Skateboards to the Capitol Hill Youth Center to volunteer.
beats.reasoning: Jesse volunteers on weekends — his good heart shows here. Skateboards to a nearby location, ~40 min to account for detours and his relaxed pace.

beats.activity: Volunteers at the youth center — helps with art activities, mentors kids, plays arcade games.
beats.reasoning: Jesse's Sunday volunteer shift. He's good with kids and loves the energy of the arcade bar-like environment. Stays for 2.5 hours.

beats.activity: Skateboards home through Capitol Hill, stops at a corner store for energy drinks.
beats.reasoning: Heads home as evening approaches. Stops briefly for snacks — typical Jesse detour. Sunny day, so he takes his time.

beats.activity: Draws graffiti-style art at home while blasting loud music; sketches ideas for his portfolio.
beats.reasoning: Jesse's evening routine — creative time with background noise. He's restless but channeling it into art. Gets anxious in quiet spaces, so music is essential.

beats.activity: Winds down — scrolls social media, texts friends, puts on a podcast to fall asleep to.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat before sleep. Jesse stays home and gradually transitions to bedtime. Background noise (podcast) helps him relax.

diary: Woke up late-ish and cruised to Victrola on the board—perfect weather for it. The shop was buzzing, which is always good. Made a ton of espresso drinks, talked to this regular who's been coming in forever about her garden. Took a quick break outside around 11, scrolled for a bit. Stayed late helping restock because honestly I wasn't in a rush to leave. Grabbed a burger at Dick's after and headed to the youth center. The kids were wild today—we did some spray paint art, and this one girl actually nailed a piece she'd been working on. Felt good, you know? Skateboarded home through the neighborhood as the sun was still up, grabbed some energy drinks. Spent the evening drawing in my room, just letting the music blare and getting lost in sketches. Feeling pretty solid about where some of these designs are heading for my portfolio.
thought_process: Today felt like a good balance—work was steady and the tips were decent, which always helps my mood. I wasn't really thinking about the World Cup match or the light rail closure since I stick to skateboarding anyway. What got me was the time at the youth center; mentoring those kids reminds me why I'm trying to build my art skills in the first place. I could've just gone home after work, but staying late at Victrola felt right, and the volunteer shift energized me instead of draining me. By evening I was in a creative headspace, so I used it. The music and sketching helped me decompress and feel like I'm actually working toward something real with my art.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
